text:
#### 2. Internet Banking Platform (IBP)  
**System ID:** IBP-001
**Purpose:** Web-based customer portal for account access and self-service
**Technology:** React.js frontend, Node.js backend
**Deployment:** Cloud (AWS)
**Availability:** 99.8% (SLA)  
**Key Features:**
- Secure login (username/password + OTP)
- Account information dashboard
- Transaction history and search
- Standing orders and direct debits
- Beneficiary management
- Money transfer
- E-statements
- Profile and settings management  
**Security:**
- TLS 1.3 encryption
- Session management (15-min idle timeout)
- Two-factor authentication (OTP via email/SMS)
- XSS/CSRF protection
- SQL injection prevention  
**Usage:**
- Daily active users: 200–300
- Peak traffic: 10:00–12:00 GMT
- Response time: <2 seconds (p95)  
**Integration:**
- Account Management System (AMS) — account data
- Fraud Detection Engine — transaction validation
- Payment Processing System (PPS) — transfer execution  
---
name: Chunk 5/39

raw_entities.name: Internet Banking Platform
raw_entities.description: A cloud-deployed (AWS) web-based customer portal built on React.js and Node.js, designed to provide account access, self-service capabilities, and secure transaction management for retail customers.
context: Web-based customer portal for account access and self-service

raw_entities.name: Account Management System
raw_entities.description: An integrated backend system that supplies core account data to the Internet Banking Platform for dashboard display and customer self-service operations.

raw_entities.name: Fraud Detection Engine
raw_entities.description: A security integration responsible for validating transactions initiated through the customer portal to prevent unauthorized or suspicious financial activity.

raw_entities.name: Payment Processing System
raw_entities.description: An external infrastructure system integrated to execute money transfers and standing orders initiated by customers via the web portal.

raw_entities.name: Platform Availability SLA
raw_entities.description: A service level agreement requirement mandating 99.8% operational uptime for the Internet Banking Platform to ensure continuous customer access.

raw_entities.name: Daily Active User Capacity
raw_entities.description: An operational capacity metric defining the expected daily user base range for the platform, measured between 200 and 300 active users.

raw_entities.name: Peak Traffic Window
raw_entities.description: A defined operational period indicating maximum system load, occurring daily between 10:00 and 12:00 GMT.

raw_entities.name: Response Time Threshold
raw_entities.description: A performance SLA requiring all platform interactions to complete in under 2 seconds at the 95th percentile (p95) to maintain user experience standards.

raw_entities.name: Account
raw_entities.description: A core banking entity whose information is displayed on the customer dashboard and accessed via the web portal for self-service management.

raw_entities.name: Transaction
raw_entities.description: A financial event recorded in customer history, subject to search functionality and mandatory fraud validation before execution.

raw_entities.name: Standing Order
raw_entities.description: A recurring payment instruction managed through the self-service portal, requiring beneficiary setup and scheduled execution.

raw_entities.name: Direct Debit
raw_entities.description: An automated payment authorization feature available for customer management within the platform, enabling recurring collections.

raw_entities.name: Customer
raw_entities.description: The end-user entity accessing the web portal to manage profiles, view account information, and initiate transfers or beneficiary updates.

raw_entities.name: Session Idle Timeout Constraint
raw_entities.description: A security control mandating automatic session termination after 15 minutes of inactivity to protect customer accounts from unauthorized access.

raw_entities.name: Two-Factor Authentication Requirement
raw_entities.description: A mandatory security protocol requiring OTP verification via email or SMS for all secure login attempts to the platform.

raw_entities.name: Encryption Standard Compliance
raw_entities.description: A data protection rule enforcing TLS 1.3 encryption for all communications within the platform to safeguard sensitive customer and transactional data.

properties.purpose: Web-based customer portal for account access and self-service

properties.what_it_measures: Target uptime for the internet banking platform
Number of unique users accessing the platform daily
Daily period of highest concurrent user traffic

properties.definition: Financial record accessed and managed by customers via self-service portal
Monetary movement recorded within a customer account
Pre-authorized recurring payment instruction initiated by the customer
Payment method allowing authorized third-party collection from a customer account
Individual or entity utilizing the banking platform for self-service and account management

properties.rule_statement: User sessions must automatically terminate after 15 minutes of inactivity
All customer logins must require OTP verification via email or SMS
All data in transit must be encrypted using TLS 1.3 standard

relationships.description: IBP tracks and reports against its platform availability SLA target.
IBP monitors daily active user counts against capacity targets.
IBP identifies and tracks peak traffic periods for capacity planning.
IBP performance is evaluated against response time SLAs.
IBP provides account information dashboards and self-service access.
IBP records and displays transaction history for user review.
IBP enables customers to create and manage standing orders.
IBP enables customers to set up and manage direct debits.
IBP serves as the primary web portal for customer account access and self-service.
relationships.context: Web-based customer portal for account access

relationships.description: IBP implements automatic session termination after 15 minutes of inactivity.
IBP mandates OTP verification via email or SMS during secure login.
IBP applies TLS 1.3 encryption to secure data in transit.
IBP retrieves account data from AMS to populate dashboards.
IBP calls the Fraud Detection Engine to validate transactions before execution.
IBP delegates transfer execution to the Payment Processing System.
